# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image 
FROM python:3-slim   # Adjust this line if necessary (e.g., use 'python' instead)

# Set the working directory in the container to /app, so we can easily run docker commands there after setting up our app source code and requirements below    
WORKDIR /app 

# Copy application files from host machine into Docker image at path '/root/src/*.py' (adjust this line if necessary)   # Adjust these lines as well to match your project structure, e.g., '*.sql', '.git*','data.*'. This can be a comma-separated list
COPY src /app 
# Copy other application files/directories from host machine into Docker image at path '/root' (adjust this line if necessary)   # Adjust these lines as well to match your project structure, e.g., '*.sql', '.git*','data.*'. This can be a comma-separated list
COPY data /app 
# Install production dependencies in the app directory with nescessary permissions set up for easy development and debugging   # Adjust this line if necessary (e.g, to use 'production' instead of your package name)    # Also add any other needed packages or requirements here as well: pip install --no-cache-dir -r /root/requirements_prod.txt
RUN npm i  && \   # Adjust this line if necessary (e.g., to use 'production' instead of your package name)    # Also add any other needed packages or requirements here as well: pip install --no
